items.title: CLI
items.text: odoo-addons-path [CODEBASE] [OPTIONS]

items.title: Arguments
items.text: Argument Description Default CODEBASE Path to the Odoo project root $CODEBASE env var

items.title: Options
items.text: Option Type Description --addons-dir TEXT Comma-separated glob patterns for addon directories. Skips detector. --odoo-dir TEXT Path to Odoo source directory. Skips detector. --verbose flag Show categorized path breakdown --help flag Show help message

items.title: Exit Codes
items.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (no codebase provided, path not found, etc.)

items.title: get_addons_path
items.text:
from odoo_addons_path import get_addons_path

result: str = get_addons_path(
    codebase: Path,
    addons_dir: list[Path] | None = None,
    odoo_dir: Path | None = None,
    verbose: bool = False,
)

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description codebase Path Root directory of the Odoo project addons_dir list[Path] \| None Explicit addon paths — skips detector odoo_dir Path \| None Explicit Odoo source path — skips detector verbose bool Print categorized paths to stdout

Returns: Comma-separated string of addon paths, ready for odoo.conf.

Example:

from pathlib import Path
from odoo_addons_path import get_addons_path

addons_path = get_addons_path(Path("/srv/odoo/project"))
# "/srv/odoo/project/odoo/addons,/srv/odoo/project/addons/mymodule"

items.title: Source Modules
items.text: Module Purpose odoo_addons_path.cli Typer CLI entry point odoo_addons_path.main Core orchestration and path aggregation odoo_addons_path.detector Layout detectors (Chain of Responsibility) odoo_addons_path.__init__ Public API — exports get_addons_path

items.title: Setup
items.text:
git clone https://github.com/trobz/odoo-addons-path
cd odoo-addons-path
uv sync
make install   # install deps + pre-commit hooks

items.title: Development Commands
items.text:
make check     # lint, format, type-check
make test      # run pytest
tox            # multi-Python testing (3.10–3.13)

items.title: Adding a Layout Detector
items.text:
1. Open src/odoo_addons_path/detector.py
  2. Subclass CodeBaseDetector and implement detect()
  3. Insert your detector into the chain in main.py::_detect_codebase_layout()
  4. Add a fixture in tests/data/ matching the new layout
  5. Write tests covering detection and path output
class MyLayoutDetector(CodeBaseDetector):
    def detect(self, codebase: Path) -> DetectionResult | None:
        marker = codebase / ".my-marker"
        if not marker.exists():
            return None
        # return paths...

items.title: Commit Convention
items.text:
Releases are automated via semantic versioning:

git commit -m "feat: add new layout detector"   # MINOR bump
git commit -m "fix: handle missing manifest"     # PATCH bump
git commit -m "feat!: change public API"         # MAJOR bump

items.title: Running Tests
items.text:
# Single Python version
make test

# All supported versions
tox

Tests use real-world fixtures in tests/data/ — no mocks for filesystem layout detection.

items.title: Installation
items.text:
# Recommended: install as a global tool
uv tool install odoo-addons-path

# Or with pip
pip install odoo-addons-path

items.title: Basic Usage
items.text:
Point it at your Odoo project root — it auto-detects the layout:

odoo-addons-path /path/to/your/odoo/project

Output:

/home/project/odoo/addons,/home/project/addons/repo1,/home/project/addons/repo2

items.title: CLI Options
items.text:
# Verbose output — shows categorized paths
odoo-addons-path /path/to/project --verbose

# Explicit addons dir — detector is SKIPPED
odoo-addons-path /path/to/project --addons-dir "./addons/*/18.0,./custom"

# Explicit Odoo dir — detector is SKIPPED
odoo-addons-path /path/to/project --odoo-dir /opt/odoo

# Both explicit paths
odoo-addons-path /path/to/project --odoo-dir /opt/odoo --addons-dir "./custom"

Detector Skip Behavior

The layout detector is skipped when any explicit --addons-dir or --odoo-dir is provided. This ensures predictable, reproducible output when you specify paths manually.

items.title: Environment Variable
items.text:
export CODEBASE=/home/project
odoo-addons-path  # uses $CODEBASE automatically

items.title: Python API
items.text:
from pathlib import Path
from odoo_addons_path import get_addons_path

# Auto-detect layout
paths = get_addons_path(Path("/path/to/project"))
print(paths)
# /path/to/project/addons,/path/to/project/enterprise

# With explicit options
paths = get_addons_path(
    codebase=Path("/home/project"),
    addons_dir=[Path("/home/project/custom")],
    verbose=True,
)

items.title: Next Steps
items.text:
• Supported Layouts — see which project structures are auto-detected
  • API Reference — full CLI flags and Python API docs

items.title: Supported Layouts
items.text: odoo-addons-path uses a Chain of Responsibility pattern — detectors run in order, first match wins.

items.title: Detection Order
items.text: Priority Layout Marker Detection Method 1 Trobz .trobz/ directory Explicit directory marker 2 Camptocamp (C2C) Dockerfile with C2C label File content scan 3 Odoo.sh 4-directory structure Directory presence check 4 Doodba .copier-answers.yml YAML config key 5 Generic Any __manifest__.py Recursive filesystem search

items.title: Trobz
items.text:
Identified by the presence of a .trobz/ directory at the project root.

project/
├── .trobz/
├── odoo/
└── addons/
    ├── repo1/
    └── repo2/

items.title: Camptocamp (C2C)
items.text:
Identified by a Dockerfile containing a Camptocamp-specific label.

project/
├── Dockerfile        # contains C2C label
├── odoo/
└── addons/

Both classic (c2c) and new (c2c-new) layout variants are supported.

items.title: Odoo.sh
items.text:
Identified by the standard 4-directory structure used on the Odoo.sh platform.

project/
├── odoo/
├── enterprise/
├── design-themes/
└── src/

items.title: Doodba
items.text:
Identified by a .copier-answers.yml file with Doodba-specific configuration keys.

project/
├── .copier-answers.yml
├── odoo/
│   └── custom/
│       └── src/
└── ...

items.title: Generic (Fallback)
items.text:
When no known layout is detected, falls back to a recursive search for any directory containing __manifest__.py files.

project/
└── any-structure/
    └── my_module/
        └── __manifest__.py   # ← found by recursive search

items.title: Version Detection
items.text: Starting from v1.2.0, the tool also detects Odoo version from module manifests and reports inconsistencies when --verbose is used.

items.title: Test Fixtures
items.text: Real-world layout fixtures are available in tests/data/ for reference: trobz, c2c, c2c-new, doodba, odoo-sh, repo-version-module
